concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have associated numerical values (weights).

concepts.name: Connected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where there is a path between any two vertices.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected acyclic graph.

concepts.name: Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph that is a tree and connects all vertices of the original graph.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A spanning tree with the minimum possible total edge weight.

concepts.name: Prim's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A greedy algorithm for finding a Minimum Spanning Tree.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Edge Weight
concepts.short_definition: The numerical value assigned to an edge in a weighted graph.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit of a graph, representing a point or entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate connections between vertices.

concepts.name: Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores elements with associated priorities and allows efficient retrieval of the element with the highest (or lowest) priority.

concepts.name: Key Value
concepts.short_definition: The weight associated with a vertex in Prim's algorithm, representing the minimum weight of an edge connecting it to the MST.

concepts.name: Parent Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer from a vertex to its predecessor in the MST construction.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of vertices that have already been included in the MST.

concepts.name: Cut Property
concepts.short_definition: A property stating that for any cut of a graph, the minimum weight edge crossing the cut must be part of some MST.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each vertex in a graph.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors level by level.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Union
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that keeps track of a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets.

concepts.name: Kruskal's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: Another greedy algorithm for finding a Minimum Spanning Tree.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Different ways to store graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Edge Relaxation
concepts.short_definition: The process of updating the key value of a vertex if a shorter path is found.

concepts.name: Initialization
concepts.short_definition: The process of setting up initial values for data structures before an algorithm begins.

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A single pass or repetition of a process within an algorithm.

concepts.name: Termination Condition
concepts.short_definition: The condition that determines when an algorithm should stop executing.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Subgraphs where any two vertices are connected to each other by paths, and which are connected to no additional vertices in the supergraph.

concepts.name: Acyclicity
concepts.short_definition: The property of a graph not containing any cycles.

concepts.name: Graph Algorithms
concepts.short_definition: Algorithms designed to operate on graph data structures.

concepts.name: Optimization Problem
concepts.short_definition: A problem where the goal is to find the best solution from a set of possible solutions.

concepts.name: Network Flow
concepts.short_definition: A concept related to directed graphs with capacities on edges, often used in optimization problems.

concepts.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for finding the shortest paths between nodes in a graph.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices in a graph with the minimum total edge weight.

concepts.name: Cut
concepts.short_definition: A partition of the vertices of a graph into two disjoint sets.
